nodes.name: server.py
nodes.summary: FastMCP server that dynamically loads an OpenAPI spec (from OPENAPI_SPEC_URL or OPENAPI_SPEC_FILE env var) and registers every API endpoint as a distinct MCP tool at startup. Uses a closure pattern to bind each handler to its operation ID.

nodes.name: loader.py
nodes.summary: Loads an OpenAPI spec from either a remote URL (via httpx) or a local file path. Supports both JSON and YAML formats with auto-detection by content-type or file extension.

nodes.name: parser.py
nodes.summary: Parses an OpenAPI 3.x spec dict into a list of Endpoint dataclasses. Extracts base URL from the servers block, iterates all path/method combinations, and resolves path, query, header, and request body parameters.

nodes.name: executor.py
nodes.summary: Executes an HTTP request for a given Endpoint with provided arguments. Resolves path parameters, query params, headers, and JSON body from argument dict. Reads auth config from AUTH_HEADER_NAME/VALUE or API_BEARER_TOKEN env vars.

nodes.name: test_executor.py
nodes.summary: Tests for the call_endpoint executor function, verifying path substitution, query params, and auth header injection.

nodes.name: test_loader.py
nodes.summary: Tests for load_spec, covering local JSON/YAML files and HTTP URL fetching with mocked httpx.

nodes.name: test_parser.py
nodes.summary: Tests for parse_spec, verifying that endpoints, parameters, and base URLs are extracted correctly from sample specs.

nodes.name: Endpoint
nodes.summary: Dataclass representing a single API endpoint with operation_id, method, path, summary, description, params list, and tags.

nodes.name: EndpointParam
nodes.summary: Dataclass representing one parameter of an endpoint, with name, location (path/query/header/body), required flag, description, and type.

nodes.name: register_tools
nodes.summary: Loads a spec and dynamically registers every endpoint as a FastMCP tool using closures to capture the operation ID for each handler.

nodes.name: load_spec
nodes.summary: Fetches or reads an OpenAPI spec and returns it as a parsed Python dict.

nodes.name: parse_spec
nodes.summary: Converts an OpenAPI spec dict into (base_url, [Endpoint]) by iterating all paths and HTTP methods.

nodes.name: call_endpoint
nodes.summary: Async function that makes the actual HTTP call for an endpoint, resolving all parameter locations and attaching auth headers.

tours.title: How OpenAPI Endpoints Become MCP Tools

steps.reason: Start here — main() reads the spec source env var and calls register_tools.
The spec is fetched from URL or file; supports JSON and YAML.
The loaded dict is turned into a list of typed Endpoint objects.
Each Endpoint gets a dynamically created FastMCP tool via closures.

tours.title: How a Tool Call Executes an API Request

steps.reason: Understand the data structure passed to the executor.
Each parameter carries its location (path/query/header/body) to drive request construction.
The executor resolves all parameters, adds auth headers, and makes the async HTTP call.
